sections.paragraph:
fut. יָשֹׁק (cogn. to שׁוּק).-

(1) to run up and down, to run about, used especially of those who eagerly seek any thing; used of locusts [?], Joe 2:9 Isa 33:4 (followed by בְּ of prey); hence-

(2) to be eager, greedy, thirsty, used of a bear, Pro 28:15 of a thirsty man, Isa 29:8 Psa 107:9.

Hithpalpal הִשְׁתַּקְשַׁק i.q. Kal No. 1; Nah 2:5.

Derivative מַשָּׁק.
